title: festuca halleri var. aurata
intro: Festuca halleri var. aurata, also known as Golden Fescue, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various health issues, including inflammation and skin conditions. The plant is known to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce pain, swelling, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Golden Fescue can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
